body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
irrlicht_observes: n/a

## Reasoning

The `autonomous-loop` scenario exercises the `autonomous_mode` primitive: the agent is given a goal/condition and self-prompts through internally-generated turns until the goal is met, with no user prompt separating iterations (Codex `/goal`, Plandex full-auto, SWE-agent, Goose recipe). Aider 0.86.2 has no such feature.

**No goal-driven / self-prompting surface in aider 0.86.2:**

- No `/goal`, `--auto`, `--autonomous`, or `--full-auto`. `aider --help` exposes only `--auto-commits`, `--auto-lint`, `--auto-test`, `--auto-accept-architect`, and `--yes-always` — all of which suppress *confirmation prompts*; none drive a self-prompting loop toward a goal.
- The in-chat slash commands (`/code`, `/ask`, `/architect`, `/run`, `/test`, `/lint`, …) are all single-shot; none registers a re-prompting hook. There is no `/goal` equivalent.
- A keyword scan of the installed aider package (`grep -riE 'autonomous|self.?prompt|full.?auto|goal|swe.?agent'`) surfaced only substring false positives (`prompt_pending`, `prompt_session` in `io.py`/`gui.py`). No autonomous-loop machinery exists.

**What aider *does* have is a bounded self-correction loop, which is NOT the scenario.** `Coder.run_one` (`coders/base_coder.py`) wraps `send_message` in a `while message:` loop driven by `self.reflected_message`, hard-capped at `max_reflections = 3` ("Only 3 reflections allowed, stopping."). The only things that set `reflected_message` are *technical failures*: a malformed edit block, an auto-lint error (`--auto-lint`), an auto-test failure (`--auto-test`), a needs-to-add-files message, or an edit-application exception. This is post-edit error recovery, not iteration toward a user-stated goal until `goal_status.met == true`. It is bounded (≤3), failure-triggered, and terminates on success — the opposite of the open-ended self-prompting the scenario requires.

**Architect mode is a fixed two-step chain, not a loop.** `ArchitectCoder.reply_completed` runs the architect model once to plan, then — gated on `confirm_ask("Edit the files?")` unless `--auto-accept-architect` — spawns one editor coder to apply, then returns to the prompt. That is the "limited multi-step" the prior matrix note referred to; it is two model calls within a single user turn, with no goal condition and no self-re-prompting. It does not satisfy `autonomous_mode`.

The upstream HISTORY/changelog confirms the design intent: aider is an "AI pair programming" tool that "consistently requires user confirmation between iterations"; the only autonomy is bounded post-edit self-correction (auto-lint/test/reflection), explicitly scoped to immediate technical failures rather than multi-step goal pursuit.

## Observability (n/a)

Because aider lacks the feature, `irrlicht_observes` is `n/a` — there is nothing to observe. For completeness: aider's transport is `FilesUnderCWD` over the markdown `.aider.chat.history.md`, and the adapter parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/parser.go`) opens a turn on a `#### ` user-prompt line and closes it via `IdleFlush` after 1500ms of transcript quiet. Even the reflection loop is invisible to this transport: reflections emit no `#### ` line, so the daemon would see a single working span that ends on idle — it could never distinguish a 3-reflection self-correction from one long model call. But this is moot: the agent does not support the scenario, so the pipeline is frozen at Stage 1 with no recording.

## Capability key

The scenario's **Primitive exercised** maps to `autonomous_mode`. `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` has no `autonomous_mode` key (and no agent's capabilities.json declares one as a literal flag — it is the conceptual primitive). Aider's nearest flags are `headless_mode: true` (the scenario `requires`) and the `--auto-*` confirmation-suppression switches, none of which is autonomous self-prompting.
caveats: Aider's bounded reflection loop (max_reflections=3, failure-triggered) is the closest thing to autonomy but is error-recovery, not goal-driven self-prompting; do not mistake it for autonomous_mode in future re-assessments.
Architect mode's plan→edit is a fixed two-step chain within one user turn, not a loop — this is the 'limited multi-step' the prior matrix note flagged; it does not satisfy the scenario.

sources.note: run_one() reflection loop: `while message:` re-prompts on self.reflected_message, hard-capped at max_reflections=3 ('Only 3 reflections allowed, stopping.'). reflected_message is set only by lint/test/edit-failure paths (lines ~1606/1622/2315/2327) — bounded self-correction, not goal-driven autonomy.

ArchitectCoder.reply_completed: single architect plan then one editor coder to apply, gated on confirm_ask('Edit the files?') unless --auto-accept-architect. Fixed two-step, no goal condition, no self-re-prompt.

sources.ref: aider --help (aider 0.86.2 binary at /Users/ingo/.local/bin/aider) + commands.py slash-command scan
sources.note: Only --auto-commits/--auto-lint/--auto-test/--auto-accept-architect/--yes-always (confirmation suppressors); slash commands /code /ask /architect /run /test /lint etc. are single-shot. No /goal, --auto, --autonomous, or --full-auto. grep -riE 'autonomous|self.?prompt|full.?auto|goal|swe.?agent' over the package found only prompt_pending/prompt_session false positives.

Changelog/design: aider 'consistently requires user confirmation between iterations'; only autonomy is bounded post-edit self-correction (auto-lint/test/reflection) scoped to immediate technical failures, not multi-step goal pursuit. No autonomous/agentic-loop feature shipped.

Transport read for completeness: FilesUnderCWD markdown; turn opens on '#### ' user line, closes via IdleFlush after 1500ms quiet. Reflections write no '#### ' line so would be invisible anyway — moot since agent_supports is no.
